FREELANCE SERVICE AGREEMENT

This Freelance Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 1 March 2026,
between Creative Agency Ltd, a company registered in the United Kingdom
("Client"), and the individual service provider ("Freelancer").

1. SERVICES
The Freelancer agrees to design and develop a marketing website comprising up to
five (5) pages as directed by the Client. Additional pages or features not specified
herein may be requested by the Client at any time, at no additional charge, at the
Client's sole discretion.

2. PAYMENT
The Client shall pay the Freelancer a fixed fee of £4,500 for the completed project.
Payment terms are Net 90 days from the date of invoice. No late payment interest
shall apply. The Client may withhold payment indefinitely if the deliverables are
deemed unsatisfactory at the Client's sole discretion.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
Upon execution of this Agreement, all work product, designs, code, and materials
created by the Freelancer shall immediately become the sole and exclusive property
of the Client. This transfer of ownership takes effect regardless of whether payment
has been made.

4. TERMINATION
The Client may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason,
by providing two (2) hours' written notice to the Freelancer. In the event of
termination, the Freelancer shall not be entitled to any payment for work completed
or in progress.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY
The Freelancer agrees to keep all Client information strictly confidential. This
obligation applies to all information, whether or not designated as confidential,
and survives termination of this Agreement indefinitely. No equivalent obligation
applies to the Client with respect to the Freelancer's information.

6. LIABILITY
The Freelancer shall be solely and unlimitedly liable for any and all damages,
losses, claims, or expenses arising out of or related to this Agreement, including
but not limited to indirect, incidental, consequential, and punitive damages.
The Client's liability to the Freelancer shall not exceed £100 under any
circumstances.

7. WARRANTIES
The Freelancer warrants that all deliverables will be free from defects for a
period of thirty (30) days after delivery. Acceptance criteria shall be determined
solely by the Client at the time of review.

8. AMENDMENTS
The Client reserves the right to amend any term of this Agreement at any time
without notice to the Freelancer. Continued provision of services constitutes
acceptance of any amendments.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of
the State of Delaware, United States. Any disputes shall be resolved exclusively
in the courts of Delaware, USA.

10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
All disputes arising under this Agreement shall be resolved solely through litigation
in the courts of Delaware. No alternative dispute resolution mechanisms shall apply.

11. RENEWAL
This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive twelve (12) month periods
unless either party provides written notice of termination at least three (3)
business days before the renewal date.

12. DELIVERABLES
The Freelancer will deliver a fully responsive, cross-browser-compatible marketing
website including: homepage, about page, services page, blog, and contact page.
Each deliverable will include a written acceptance checklist with objective criteria
that both parties must sign off before the next phase begins.

13. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for failure to perform its obligations under
this Agreement due to circumstances beyond its reasonable control, including but
not limited to acts of God, government actions, pandemics, power outages, or
internet service disruptions. In such cases, the affected party must notify the
other in writing within 48 hours and provide a revised timeline.

14. DATA PROTECTION
The Client confirms it is the data controller for any personal data processed under
this Agreement. The Freelancer agrees to process personal data only as instructed by
the Client and to implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures
in accordance with applicable data protection law, including GDPR where applicable.

15. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes
all prior negotiations, representations, or agreements. Any variation must be made
in writing — signed by both parties.

---
Client signature: ___________________     Date: _____________
Freelancer signature: _______________     Date: _____________
